scenarios.description: load() returns DotenvImpl when systemProperties is false and no entries are returned (branch-false, loop-0)
scenarios.precondition: Mock DotenvParser.parse() to return an empty list; mocking necessary because parser is constructed internally
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Dotenv instance is DotenvImpl; entries() returns only system environment variables; no new system properties are set
pseudocode: TEST TC01 load without systemProperties and empty entries

scenarios.description: load() sets system properties when systemProperties is true but no entries (branch-true, loop-0)
scenarios.precondition: Mock DotenvParser.parse() to return an empty list; builder.systemProperties() enabled
scenarios.expectedResult: returned Dotenv instance is DotenvImpl; entries() still contains only system environment variables; no extra system properties set beyond existing
pseudocode: TEST TC02 load with systemProperties and empty entries

scenarios.description: load() sets one system property when systemProperties is true and parser returns one entry (branch-true, loop-1)
scenarios.precondition: Mock DotenvParser.parse() to return a single-entry list; builder.systemProperties() enabled
scenarios.expectedResult: System.getProperty("KEY1") equals "VALUE1"; returned DotenvImpl.entries() contains that entry plus system env
pseudocode: TEST TC03 load with systemProperties and single entry

scenarios.description: load() sets multiple system properties when systemProperties is true and parser returns multiple entries (branch-true, loop-N)
scenarios.precondition: Mock DotenvParser.parse() to return multiple entries; builder.systemProperties() enabled
scenarios.expectedResult: All keys "A","B","C" have System.getProperty equal to "1","2","3" respectively; result.entries() contains all entries
pseudocode: TEST TC04 load with systemProperties and multiple entries
List<DotenvEntry> list = List.of(new DotenvEntry("A","1"), new DotenvEntry("B","2"), new DotenvEntry("C","3"));

scenarios.description: load() propagates DotenvException when parser.parse() throws (exception)
scenarios.precondition: Mock DotenvParser.parse() to throw new DotenvException("fail"); mocking needed to simulate parse failure
scenarios.expectedResult: throws DotenvException with message "fail"; no system properties set
pseudocode: TEST TC05 load throws DotenvException on parse failure
